name: Whole Wheat Dill Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. Warm together the water; cottage cheese, yogurt, sugar, dried onion, and oil in a microwave for about 30 seconds or until warm to the touch (about 110°F). Set aside.
2. Place all other ingredients into the bread pan in the order listed. Add the warmed ingredients.
3. Select the white bread setting on your machine; other brands of mixers may have a whole wheat setting, which works well with the DAK. Set the light/dark setting on the side of the mixer to 11 o'clock.
4. Press Start. After the dough has kneaded for about 2 minutes, open the lid.
5. Press the dough lightly with your fingertip. If the wet, sticky dough adheres to your fingertip or if the dough is not forming into one soft ball (no sticky dough on the bottom of the pan), add 1 tablespoon or more flour until the dough is just slightly tacky and makes one soft ball.
6. Once the desired consistency is reached, let the bread rest for a few minutes before baking.
7. Slice the bread top to bottom first, then place the two cut sides down and slice them thin. This makes a great sandwich bread or is good toasted with a little diet margarine spread.
Note: Best enjoyed fresh.
